summary: Tested Kundenportal (Self-Service-Portal) feature. Fixed critical auth bug where get_current_user wasn't extracting token from Authorization header. Fixed frontend api.js to send token in Authorization header instead of query params. All 21 backend tests pass. Frontend UI verified: portal creation, detail view, public customer portal login/upload.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/portals - creates portal with token, password, expiry
Backend: POST /api/portals/{id}/upload - admin uploads file to portal
Backend: GET /api/portals/{id}/files - lists files in portal
Backend: POST /api/portal/verify/{token} - verifies with correct password
Backend: POST /api/portal/{token}/files - lists files with password
Frontend: Kundenportale nav item visible in sidebar
Frontend: Portal list page loads correctly
Frontend: Create portal dialog opens and works
Frontend: Portal detail view shows files, link, password
Frontend: Public customer portal login page renders
Frontend: Wrong password shows error message
Frontend: Correct password shows upload section
seed_data_creation: Created test portal 'Test Kunde UI' with password 'testkunde2024' for UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Kundenportal feature fully implemented and tested. Key fixes applied: 1) auth.py now extracts token from Authorization header using Header() dependency, 2) frontend api.js now sends token in Authorization header instead of query params. Portal endpoints: Admin routes require JWT auth (Authorization: Bearer {token}), public routes (/api/portal/*) don't require auth but need password in request body. Object storage (Emergent) integration working for file uploads/downloads.
rca_of_the_issue: Auth was broken because get_current_user(token: str = None) expected token as a function parameter but FastAPI Depends() doesn't pass query params to dependencies. Fixed by using Header() to extract from Authorization header. Frontend was sending token as query param which also didn't work with the new auth.
